title: senecillis wilsoniana
intro: Senecillis wilsoniana, also known as Wilson's germander, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers contain a compound called juncin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits including improved digestion and reduced risk of infections. Senecillis wilsoniana can be prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal remedies, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
